stem: A HR analytics study surveys 20 staff members and observes 1 positive outcomes out of 20 (5.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (10 observations, rate = 10.0%) and Group B (10 observations, rate = 10.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 43% and 59% of the population. Estimate the true employee turnover rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.05. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.0669 (range [0.0617, 0.0721]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0089, 0.2361]. Point estimate: 0.0669.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.51: 0.51×0.035 + 0.49×0.1 = 0.0669.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
